channel.title: Bone - Health on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Bone - Health on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Vitamin D deficiency in children: 9%, but up to 75% in children with fractures
item.description:
Vitamin D and Pediatric Bone Health – Important Information and Considerations for the Pediatric Orthopaedic Surgeon

Journal of the Pediatric Orthopaedic Society of North America https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jposna.2024.100042

Fatima Bouftas a, Clarabelle DeVries b

Highlights

  • Vitamin D plays a vital role in musculoskeletal health and optimizing vitamin D levels in childhood and adolescence is crucial to proper bone development.

  • Vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency in the general population is common; 9% of the pediatric population , representing 7.6 million US children and adolescents, are vitamin D deficient and 61%, representing 50.8 million US children and adolescents, are vitamin D insufficient. The prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in the pediatric fracture population can be as high as 75%.

item.title: Bone resorption during pregnancy reduced by weekly 28,000 IU of Vitamin D
item.description:
Prenatal but not continued postpartum vitamin D supplementation reduces maternal bone resorption as measured by C-terminal telopeptide of type 1 collagen without effects on other biomarkers of bone metabolism

Endocrine and Metabolic Science, Volume 14, 31 March 2024, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.endmts.2023.100154

Christine Krupa a, Huma Qamar a, Karen M. O’Callaghan a b, Akpevwe Onoyovwi a, Abdullah Al Mahmud c, Tahmeed Ahmed c, Alison D. Gernand d, Daniel E. Roth a e f

Highlights

  • Prenatal 28,000 IU vitamin D3/week reduced CTx at delivery, compared to placebo.

item.title: 50,000 IU of Vitamin D weekly greatly decreased bone loss from prostate cancer therapy - RCT
item.description:
High-dose vitamin D to attenuate bone loss in patients with prostate cancer on androgen deprivation therapy: A phase 2 RCT

Cancer . 2024 Mar 23. doi: 10.1002/cncr.35275 PDF is behind a paywall

Luke J Peppone 1 2, Amber S Kleckner 3, Chunkit Fung 4, J Edward Puzas 2, Jennifer E Reschke 1, Eva Culakova 1, Julia Inglis 5, Charles Kamen 1, Jonathan W Friedberg 4, Michelle Janelsins 1, Karen Mustian 1, Charles E Heckler 1, Supriya Mohile 4
